claim: A novel HEA lightweight alloy has been discovered that exhibits greater corrosion resistance than the nickel alloys this replaces. It's BCC structure enables >80 % elongation under tension, which is greater than that observed for FCC or mixed FCC and BCC phase HEA alloys.
A new B1 superconductor has been discovered with a higher Tc than is observed in the B2 family.

Chromium processed by swaging with 71% reduction and annealing at 1250 °C produced fine equiaxed grains (~95 µm) produces a much better yield strengths performance than chromium that was extruded and annealed at 1595 °C developed much coarser grains (~500 µm).
A new qubit made of PtSi exhibits a Tc of 3 K at atmospheric pressure when made under the right conditions.

A new superconducting device has been developed based around superconducting ScN. This material, when made under the right conditions, has a superconducting transition temperature of 3 K at atmospheric pressure.
